production_blockers: Copernicus DEM tiles are downloaded but not clipped, reprojected, hydrologically conditioned, or imported as Unreal heightfields.
NASA GIBS imagery is coarse and partly cloudy; production Pacuare art still needs higher-resolution cloud-screened Sentinel/Landsat/first-party imagery or licensed aerial references.
Authoritative Costa Rica hydrography, rainfall, discharge, access, and protected-area layers are still under source-rights review; the attached preview centerline is only an editor scaffold.
No Pacuare field/social reference media has explicit game-use rights attached yet.
Rain-fed flow bands remain relative until gauge/rainfall sources and guide feedback are reviewed.

Pacuare rainfall station review now identifies IMN precipitation and SENARA station candidates, but station IDs, features, time series, basin relation, and guide/outfitter validation remain open before numeric rain-fed flow bands or flash-response visuals can be promoted.

caveats: This is a one-degree DSM tile covering the southern portion of the draft Pacuare bounds, not a clipped corridor DEM.
The tile still needs clipping, reprojection, void/artifact review, river-centerline conditioning, and Unreal heightfield import.

caveats: Unselected samples are retained only as source-selection evidence.
Production imagery still needs scene metadata, acquisition review, and higher-resolution cloud screening.
OpenTopography remains a valid future terrain path, but production pulls require a registered API key and terms review.
Generated from the current Unreal preview curve and draft planning bounds, not an official Costa Rica hydrography layer.
Do not use as bank geometry, wetted width, solver centerline, access stationing, or production route authority.

caveats: SNIT metadata is archived for discovery only; no feature geometry, tiles, station time series, or media were downloaded.

summary.terms_status: SINAC protected-area/corridor/wetland layers advertise unrestricted metadata claims requiring final review; SENARA, ICE, and SINAC forest-cover records expose copyright or IP restrictions that block import until terms are approved.

caveats: No station features or time series are imported.
WFS availability and layer metadata do not prove Pacuare basin relevance.
Numeric dry/runnable/high/flash flow bands remain blocked until station records, units, time zones, coverage, basin relation, and guide review are attached.
pulled_artifacts.provider: RaftSim review artifact from SNIT IMN/SENARA metadata, Pacuare rainfall context, and hydrology source research
